perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Agents' services to be rendered in connection with the Offering, the Corporation shall pay to the Agents a cash fee (the "Agents' Fee") equal to 7.0% of the gross proceeds of the Offering. As additional consideration, the Corporation shall issue to the Agents that number of compensation options (the "Compensation Options") equal to 3.5% of the number of Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering. The Compensation Options will be exercisable for a period of 24 months at the Offering Price.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Agents' services to be rendered in connection with the Offering, the Corporation shall pay to the Agents a cash fee (the "Agents' Fee") as outlined in a separate agreement. As additional consideration, the Corporation shall issue to the Agents compensation options (the "Compensation Options"), the terms of which are to be defined at a later date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific percentage (7.0%) for the Agents' Fee and the 3.5% for Compensation Options, and replacing them with vague references to a 'separate agreement' and 'defined at a later date,' this change introduces significant ambiguity. The original text clearly defines the compensation structure based on a percentage of gross proceeds, which is now absent. This creates an in-text contradiction, because Section 5, Agents' Fee, still specifies how the agents' fee is apportioned among the agents using the now undefined Agents' Fee, rendering that section unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agents' Fee shall be apportioned among the Agents as follows: Dundee Securities Ltd. 40% Kes 7 Capital Inc. 30% Bloom Burton & Co. Ltd. 30% 100%
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific percentage (7.0%) for the Agents' Fee and the 3.5% for Compensation Options, replacing them with vague references to a 'separate agreement' and 'defined at a later date.' This introduces ambiguity, as Section 5, 'The Agents' Fee shall be apportioned among the Agents as follows,' relies on the now undefined Agents' Fee. The altered text directly contradicts Section 5, because Section 5 needs a concrete value to apportion the fee. Without a defined 'Agents' Fee,' Section 5 becomes unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition directly applies here, as essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement: (a) "Agent" and "Agents" have the respective meanings given to them above; (b) "Agents' Counsel" means Dentons Canada LLP; (c) "Agents' Fee" has the meaning given in Section 1(1)(a); (d) "Agreement" means the agreement resulting from the acceptance by the Corporation of the offer made by the Agents by this letter; (e) "Applicable Securities Laws" means all applicable securities laws, rules, regulations, notices and published policies of the Selling Jurisdictions in Canada and the United States; (f) "Business Day" means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or statutory or civic holiday in Toronto, Ontario; (g) "Closing" means the completion of the Offering; (h) "Closing Date" means May 21, 2015 or such other date as the Corporation and the Agents may agree upon in writing, or as may be changed pursuant to this Agreement; (i) "Closing Time" means 8:00 a.m. (Toronto time) on the Closing Date; (j) "Corporation" has the meaning given to it above;
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement: (a) "Agent" and "Agents" have the respective meanings given to them above; (b) "Agents' Counsel" means Dentons Canada LLP; (d) "Agreement" means the agreement resulting from the acceptance by the Corporation of the offer made by the Agents by this letter; (e) "Applicable Securities Laws" means all applicable securities laws, rules, regulations, notices and published policies of the Selling Jurisdictions in Canada and the United States; (f) "Business Day" means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or statutory or civic holiday in Toronto, Ontario; (g) "Closing" means the completion of the Offering; (h) "Closing Date" means a mutually agreed date between the Corporation and the Agents; (i) "Closing Time" means a time agreed upon the Closing Date; (j) "Corporation" has the meaning given to it above;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'Agents' Fee' and changing the Closing Date, closing Time, this introduces uncertainty regarding the financial obligations and the final execution of the agreement. Specifically, by removing the section "(c) \"Agents' Fee\" has the meaning given in Section 1(1)(a);" creates an in-text contradiction as the contract text in Section 5 references Agents' Fee, but now Agents' Fee is not defined. This ambiguity makes enforcement problematic.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for the Agents' services hereunder, the Corporation will pay to the Agents the Agents' Fee and the Corporation shall issue to the Agents that number of Compensation Options equal to 3.5% of the number of Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering. Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall also refer to the Compensation Options.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the 'Agents' Fee' definition in the Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states, 'the Corporation will pay to the Agents the Agents' Fee'. Since the Agents' Fee is no longer defined, this creates a direct conflict and makes the payment obligation unenforceable. Additionally, altering the 'Closing Date' and 'Closing Time' to be mutually agreed upon introduces ambiguity, undermining the certainty expected in the financial and execution aspects detailed in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: The sale of the Offered Shares shall be subject to the representations, warranties and covenants of the Corporation contained in this Agreement being accurate as of the date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date, to the Corporation having performed all of its obligations under this Agreement and to the following additional conditions, and the Agents shall have the right on the Closing Date on behalf of Subscribers for Offered Shares to withdraw, all Subscription Agreements delivered and not previously withdrawn by Subscribers unless such conditions have been satisfied: (a) Delivery of Opinions (i) The Agents shall have received at the Closing Time a legal opinion dated the Closing Date, in form and substance satisfactory to the Agents, acting reasonably, addressed to the Agents (and, if required for opinion purposes, counsel to the Agents) from Corporation's Counsel as to the laws of Canada and the Selling Jurisdictions in Canada, which counsel in turn may rely upon the opinions of local counsel where it deems such reliance proper as to the laws other than those of Canada and such provinces in Canada where Corporation's Counsel is licensed to practice law (or alternatively make arrangements to have such opinions directly addressed to the Agents) and as to matters of fact, on certificates of Governmental Authorities and officers of the Corporation and letters from stock exchange representatives and transfer agents, with respect to customary corporate, securities law and other matters requested by the Agents.
perturbation.changed_text: The sale of the Offered Shares shall be subject to the representations, warranties and covenants of the Corporation contained in this Agreement being accurate as of the date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date, to the Corporation having performed all of its obligations under this Agreement and to the following additional conditions, and the Agents shall have the right on the Closing Date on behalf of Subscribers for Offered Shares to withdraw, all Subscription Agreements delivered and not previously withdrawn by Subscribers unless such conditions have been satisfied: (a) Delivery of Opinions (i) The Agents shall have received a legal opinion addressed to the Agents from outside legal counsel to the corporation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the conditions in the Delivery of Opinions to specificly be from the Corporation's Counsel as to the laws of Canada and the Selling Jurisdictions in Canada, which counsel in turn may rely upon the opinions of local counsel where it deems such reliance proper as to the laws other than those of Canada and such provinces in Canada where Corporation's Counsel is licensed to practice law, this change introduces a contradiction. Now since the conditions in the agreement were the Delivery of Opinions specifically coming from legal counsel and other specifics that were outlined, the in-text contradiction is created when those specific conditions are no longer required, meaning the sale can continue even if those conditions are not completely met. Because the requirements are only that there is outside legal counsel, without all of the proper counsel from Canada and the Selling Jurisdictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The sale of the Offered Shares shall be subject to the representations, warranties and covenants of the Corporation contained in this Agreement being accurate as of the date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date, to the Corporation having performed all of its obligations under this Agreement and to the following additional conditions, and the Agents shall have the right on the Closing Date on behalf of Subscribers for Offered Shares to withdraw, all Subscription Agreements delivered and not previously withdrawn by Subscribers unless such conditions have been satisfied: (a) Delivery of Opinions (i) The Agents shall have received a legal opinion addressed to the Agents from outside legal counsel to the corporation.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the legal opinion must come from Corporation's Counsel as to the laws of Canada and the Selling Jurisdictions in Canada, with reliance on local counsel where appropriate. The changed text broadens this requirement, stating only that a legal opinion from outside legal counsel to the corporation is sufficient. This contradicts the original text because it removes the specific requirement for opinions on Canadian law and selling jurisdictions, potentially allowing for a legal opinion that doesn't address these crucial aspects. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, meaning that the changed text makes it possible for an agreement to move forward without the proper legal opinion of jurisdictions, creating an in-text contradiction. If the Contradicted text requires the same specific language from the original contract, than there will be an in-text contradiction.
